item.title: The big C has been Crushered.
item.dc:creator: Az663

item.title: Single handedly changing gaming...
item.description: all the credit goes to this dude, he popped up on my youtube suggested and I don't even play that game... https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIqHEktjqu4
item.dc:creator: jayman0123

item.title: Before there was YouTube, there was this
item.description: It's every bit as fabulous as you think it is
item.dc:creator: zMike70z

item.title: The realpolitik of hope... (Imgur Guide to the wider geopolitical impact of the war in Ukraine)
item.description:
(Photo taken some years ago) It’s been just 19 days since the war in Ukraine began. While Russian forces grind their way towards the cities, something unexpected blossoms in the cold fields and rubble strewn streets and burned out villages, to seemingly fade and then rise again. Hope. Actual hope. We begin to ask... Can Ukraine actually ‘win’ this war? It’s an overwhelming question, and as such it demands our attention. We focus upon the small details and miss other questions. Questions caused by unseen ripples and unexpected side effects of the conflict. Questions not about the bombings or possible no fly zones, but about deeper things, often on the edge of the news. Most Viral Edit: Thank you UserSub for supporting a hard post to write. I am humbled again by your kindness.
Why things could become much scarier in places we don’t expect, and how, the longer this war goes on, the more profound the changes it will cause- changes which can be a source of fear and a source of hope. I’ve tried to keep this one as factual as possible, while highlighting the vast realm of unknowns before us. Mostly I hope to just to be able to add something informative and new to the constant coverage we all face. Hopefully it will only take the time it takes to have a coffee.
The Tactical Situation in Ukraine There is NOTHING I can say about the situation on the ground that hasn’t been said by more learned experts than me on TV/online/on Twitter/YouTube etc. Everyone has an opinion about what is happening. I ain’t gonna add to it. Why? The ONLY people who know the true picture, the only people who know exactly what is going on? They are kinda too busy firing weapons at folks to Tweet about stuff yeah? Everything beyond them is speculation.
As far as we can tell, however, the situation is finely balanced. Ukraine isn’t winning this war. Far from it. But they ain’t losing either. The Ukrainians have advantages in the north, the Russians are strongest in the south. Things have slowed down. Russia seeks to crush Ukraine with a massive systematic assault. We use maps to try and fill in the blanks but maps which show exactly where the Russians are, like this one, are great illustrations of how truth is in the eyes of the beholder.
Some of the voices who fill our airways claim that Russia is losing. Some of the voices are saying this utter chaos is kinda normal in how the Russians always fight and have always fought, they say Russia’s victory is inevitable. Again, we don’t KNOW. So all there is then, we muse, is just opinion and propaganda right?
The problem with having a problem with Propaganda We have seen posts here on Imgur which repeat the mantras of Arthur Ponsonby and US Senator Hiram Johnson. ‘The first casualty of war is truth’ yeah? This is a slogan only really worthy of a film poster and has no actual bearing on any war. Saying the ‘first casualty of war is truth’ is the realpolitik version of having the words ‘Live. Laugh. Love.’ tattooed just above your ass crack. It’s basic bitch territory.
In war, the ‘truth’ is a weapon, and the person who wields it best will have an advantage. Either it’s used against you or you use it against the enemy. We live in nice, safe, liberal nations and have the luxury of going ‘tsk! If it’s not the ‘truth’ then it’s just propaganda!’ And we can say that because no one is actively killing our children. But the second someone does start shelling your nurseries, then using propaganda becomes a moral imperative. Any person who isn’t willing to defend his family by _any_ means, including using propaganda, is not worth listening to. Any person who disdains its use as opposed to just accept that, like flying bullets, it is merely indicative of a battle taking place, is someone who simply doesn’t understand a damn thing about war.
Don’t recoil from propaganda. Don’t react like it’s a mortal sin or something. Embrace this fact, pick a side if you like, remain skeptical (always) and carry on. Making a big stink at people using propaganda is a privilege and a luxury. We here on Imgur can do so because we are not currently being harvested by modern war machines. And why say any of this? Because Ukrainian propaganda so far has been brilliant. It’s been jaw droppingly good.
There was no Ghost of Kyev. The ‘Russian ship fuck you’ sailors were captured not killed. But who cares? It worked. It was brilliant. It’s the way we see all those clips of Ukrainian soldiers on Twitter? Go look at them, go look at as many of them as you can. Notice how they are mostly showing the same thing. Small units of Ukrainians with stinger missiles. The brave few. We never saw the Ukrainian heavy battalions. Their tanks. They’re not gone. They are out there. Fighting. The Ukrainian Air Force is still somewhat intact. https://nationalinterest.org/blog/buzz/russia-still-hasnt-gained-air-superiority-over-ukraine-201170 https://www.ukrinform.net/amp/rubric-ato/3427215-ukraines-air-force-reports-aerial-battles-in-several-areas.html They are able to unleash a large number of drones still. Their anti-air is still intact.
Of course they are. And you can’t get all upset about Ukraine keeping this from us, no more than we can be upset Russia is trying to pretend they have not suffered from a breakdown in their army cohesion. In a war control over public perception is a strategic goal. It’s PART of the battlefield. Therefore we can say, without fear of contradiction, that Ukraine, in this respect, is currently kicking Russia’s ass.
The masterclass We need to recognise that Ukraine has unleashed a MASTERCLASS in information warfare over these last couple of weeks. I’m saying this now, Ukraine may lose battles, could still lose this war, could still be destroyed as a nation, but the way they have waged war over the internet, the war for perception, for gaining support, has been so good, folks will study this in the years to come. https://time.com/6155869/bellingcat-eliot-higgins-ukraine-open-source-intelligence/ There are so many moments I could mention, brilliant moments; brilliantly done. And it’s not just about what is said, but about what ISN’T said.
We were horrified that Russia sent in Chechen fighters to attack Ukraine. Most folks don’t realise that there are TWO Chechen regiments in Ukraine fighting to defend it, (the Dzhokhar Dudayev Battalion come to mind) and they see themselves as part of the still ongoing Chechen Civil War and wanna beat the crap out of their fellow countrymen. https://newlinesmag.com/reportage/chechens-fighting-chechens-in-ukraine/ They’re based in Mariupol. And you wonder why that region is still holding out? Do we NEED to know this? Ukraine doesn’t think so. Neither does Russia as they don’t wanna advertise that their pet House Chechens ain’t as good as Free Chechens.
It’s like the accusations of war crimes- we know Russia has committed war crimes as we see the evidence daily. They shell civilians. A tactic they have done repeatedly across Syria for example. We also know Ukraine kinda do ‘war crimes’ as well, but smaller ones. Remember that press conference of the Russian POW’s all saying they didn’t want to fight, and they were lied to etc. Using POW’s like that? Against the Geneva Convention. Technically a war crime. So is sharing images of POW’s on Imgur. Technically. https://www.cbc.ca/news/world/russian-pow-videos-ukraine-international-law-1.6377305 But war crimes is one of those terms that cover ALL breaches of the rules. What Ukraine did? Based on past examples, it gets a stern ‘don’t do that’ and maybe a fine. As opposed to deliberately shelling hospitals which could result in life sentences and in the past? Hanging.
Ukraine have also showed us just enough horror to get us upset but not enough to sap morale. I’m not going to get too much more into this as we have all seen examples of the effectiveness of Ukraine’s information warfare campaign. Look at it this way: Russia have been running the game on ‘disinformation and propaganda’; they had a massive head start. Russia has had _years_ to build the perception case for this. And they have lost. And it’s not ‘Western media bias’ that has defeated them. It’s Ukraine. Russia had a decade to undermine the objectivity of Western Media, and they had. But the reaction they have faced? Initially it was shock at war. But now? It’s Ukraine. The perception of the war is a strategic consideration and Ukraine has ran rings around the Russians. https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/ukrainealert/why-vladimir-putin-is-losing-the-information-war-to-ukraine/
Even Switzerland has brought sanctions against Russia. How do you say ‘you lost and can’t come back from that’ without saying it openly? Switzerland side against you. You want to see a war that changes the way warfare is waged in future? Here. Now. Ukraine. This is the gold standard as to how a nation gains global support in a war. For now...
I add this caveat. Ukraine have set the gold standard... but this information war has ONLY just started. As one writer said recently “In the middle of a so-called information war, as in the middle of a ground war, it is easy to make confident judgments too early and be seduced by simple narratives. What seems to make sense now may not make sense tomorrow. And the footage we see, no matter how indelible, could be interpreted quite differently through another’s eyes.” https://www.theatlantic.com/technology/archive/2022/03/russia-ukraine-war-propaganda/626975/ And FYI- don’t think countries like Taiwan, Iraq and others ain’t paying attention. They are. Everyone is. The Ukrainian Orange Revolution and Euromaiden revolts became the template by which all subsequent civil protest movements since have copied to some degree; BLM; the French ‘Yellow Vests’, the Hong Kong pro-Democracy protests, the Arab Spring, all have contained elements of those Ukrainian protests. The Ukrainians are once again creating a new template for others to copy. https://www.wilsoncenter.org/blog-post/hong-kong-protesters-draw-inspiration-ukraines-maidan (For a way more in-depth look at the power of the Ukrainian Information war I would recommend this French-translated-into-English review by Middle Eastern Eye https://www.middleeasteye.net/news/russia-ukraine-war-social-media-battleground-how It does raise some very interesting points even if it is somewhat focused on ‘double standards’ as opposed to ‘whose better at this’).
But all this brilliance has its limits. We are NOT all committing our forces to aid Ukraine are we? Because all the propaganda, even the best propaganda, has a foil in the form of the harshest reality check all passion ever runs into. Realpolitik. And to see the realpolitik of this war in action, we need to look at the Middle East. What surprises a few people is that the war in Ukraine is having wider geopolitical considerations. We are all focused on the European impact but it is beginning to spill over into the Middle East. Why?
OK so I’ve been posting about the Middle East for years, I’ll include a list of the posts below, but basically with America deciding it was done fighting in the region, Russia jumped in, like a creepy guy with a ponytail hitting up divorced mums on the rebound. Russian made a dramatic impact pretty fast. They joined the conflict in Syria. They managed to became power brokers in the region. They increased and improved relations with Saudi Arabia; become unspoken partners with the UAE in OPEC when it comes to votes/discussions on oil production; and become strategic partners of both Iran and Turkey. Russian influence in the Middle East has been one of Putin’s few diplomatic victories in the last decade. And that’s not even describing his biggest influence...
President Assad of Syria is still in power because of Russia. Russian warplanes were able to give him the firepower he needed. What Russia did in Syria is what they have only hinted at doing in Ukraine. They carpet bombed everything and anything that annoyed them. They recklessly and ruthlessly flattened everything that opposed Assad. But, like Putin has done time and again, he has taken a strong hand and throw it away. America (and others) have eroded and destroyed his previous influence in a matter of days. Proof? The UAE during the initial security council voting abstained from voting against Russia, saying picking a side would make things worse. The Gulf Cooperation Council (a body made up of Oman, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar) initially called for restraint from both sides. They wouldn’t condemn Russia.
Now? Well they ALL voted for the UN Resolution that places the blame totally upon Russia. The unified condemnation of Russia reflects not just a loss of influence for Russia but reflects the very real possibility that the entire Russian strategic objectives in the Middle East could be about to unravel in a way that will shake up the entire region. ( https://amp.cnn.com/cnn/2022/03/03/middleeast/russia-ukraine-gulf-unga-03-03-2022-intl/index.html ) It is Russian firepower that keeps a lid on Syria for example. The Idlib Province has seen Turkey and Russia kinda agree to keep a stalemate of sorts. Assad gets to run the rest of Syria, and attack here but in a ‘just destroy shit’ way. https://www.dailysabah.com/world/syrian-crisis/assad-regime-attacks-kindergarten-in-syrias-idlib-kills-2/amp But the war in Ukraine COULD change everything. If Russian troops in Ukraine can’t get fuel/ammunition/face logistics issues... what chance do the Russian flyboys in Syria have?
And then we get into the ‘frenemies’ relationship between Turkey and Russia. There are so many layers to this relationship that if I did a whole post I couldn’t do it justice. Turkey has done its NATO duty, condemned Russia, and is like selling Ukraine their cheap but effective Bayraktar TB2 drones; but it hasn’t closed its airspace to Russian planes nor introduced sanctions. They did evoke the 1936 Montreux Convention, which allows them to stop all warships passage through the strategic Bosphorus and Dardanelles straits linking the Mediterranean to the Black Sea, and led to them refusing the Russians permission to send 3 ships into the Med. (Although go back to the Information War part above- there are reports in the source that the President of Ukraine thanked Turkey for refusing the Russian ships before Turkey made the decision, in a successful attempt to force the Turks into a spotlight so as to force their hand- another example of how well they are playing this game). But the Turkish-Russian relationship is an area that is complicated and could go in many directions still... https://www.irishtimes.com/news/world/europe/war-has-turkey-caught-between-conflicting-international-and-national-interests-1.4820736
And we haven’t even gotten to Russian-Iranian relations. Iran needs Russia as a partner and this is why they have towed the line that the war in Ukraine is NATO’s fault. But kinda saying that under their breath... or they were. In Vienna right now Iran and America WERE on the verge of lifting sanctions on Iran in return for them scrapping their nuclear program BUT Russia just deliberately threw a spanner in the works of that deal... https://www.france24.com/en/middle-east/20220311-talks-to-revive-iran-nuclear-deal-paused-over-unresolved-russian-demands Remember Ukraine and Iran are not talking ever since that time when we all thought Iran was going to go to war with America and the Republican Guards accidentally shot down a Ukrainian passenger jet murdering all 176 on board. Iran was paying compensation to Ukraine but there were all kinds of legal shenanigans. https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/1/7/two-years-on-no-resolution-over-downing-of-flight-ps752
Supposedly Russia is offering good money for Syrians to fly to Ukraine and fight for them. True? Not true? We don’t know. It’s alleged. But the guys they would recruit IF they are, would weaken Assad. And also? The guys they would be getting are very good at fighting rednecks in Toyota trucks like them. If they threw any Syrian at well trained Ukrainian professional soldiers they would be mulched. So any Syrians Russia DOES recruit (if it’s true?) would be either canon fodder OR being recruited for later counter insurgency operations against an imagined Ukrainian civil resistance force. OR it’s just one of those ‘things Russia says’ to make them sound tougher then they actually are and the entire thing is meaningless. https://asia.nikkei.com/Politics/Ukraine-war/Russia-offers-Middle-East-fighters-400-a-week-to-join-Ukraine-war And https://m.jpost.com/breaking-news/article-701018
So, the longer the war in Ukraine goes on, the weaker Russia becomes in the Middle East and the more local nations may start calculating with Russia gone they need to react quickly... and change their positions on things. See my point? The fighting in Ukraine has thrown out the newly established status quo in the Middle East. The impact of it on Syria, on Libya, is yet to be determined. The whole thing? It’s complicated. https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/blogs/menasource/russias-invasion-of-ukraine-impacts-the-middle-east-too/ And my words above are merely a crude simplistic guide.
But if you want an insight into how it’s changing things... Israel have been tapped as possible peace makers. Allow me say that again, just so EVERYONE allows that line sink in. Israel have been tapped by the French and the Germans as backdoor peace negotiators with Putin. Israel?! https://m.economictimes.com/news/international/world-news/israel-pm-naftali-bennett-returns-from-surprise-trip-to-russia/amp_articleshow/90029038.cms This fact, the sheer amazement of this, is not lost on anyone. Least of all on the Israelis themselves who you can almost sense the ‘what the hell’ impact of this. https://m.jpost.com/israel-news/article-700430/amp And more recently https://www.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-13-22/h_320b54b9749356940f7387e64f294131
Oddly enough it seems to be both Turkey and Israel kinda acting like peace makers behind the scenes and while it probably won’t amount to much, it HAS started Israel and Turkey talking again and Lord knows where that could lead to... but wait a minute... Israel and Turkey are actually talking again? That I didn’t see coming... https://www.haaretz.com/israel-news/.premium-turkey-and-israel-are-in-a-race-to-mediate-between-ukraine-and-russia-1.10658547 I could speculate where this could lead to but it’s pointless. It’s too soon. There are too many possibilities. Bottom line- The war in Ukraine has caused a state of geopolitical flux in the Middle East. In a state of flux _nothing_ is sure.
Realpolitik 101 There was an article I saw written in an Israeli newspaper recently- an opinion piece, like the million or so we have seen recently. No big thing. What drew my attention to it was it was the most perfect embodiment of realpolitik as I have ever seen. The article was cold; it disdained any politics; it said people must prepare for the fact that ‘what is unimaginable today becomes inevitable tomorrow’. In a state of flux, any state of flux, this is our new mantra... what is unimaginable today becomes inevitable tomorrow. What was it the other guy said earlier? ‘What seems to make sense now may not make sense tomorrow’. Same conclusion, differing specialities, but showing how this flux is spreading.
The opinion price goes into offer advice for Israel and I would have ignored it at this point. But it’s power however lies in the fact that the advice given could apply to every nation in the world right now. It suggests we need to... “get rid of the tendency to see the nearby and distant world in black and white, and to be prepared for a situation where players assemble for a present need, and an ad hoc combination of interests dictates changes that are surprising and incomprehensible at first glance, as well as unexpected strategic developments’. This is realpolitik made real. There is no good or evil. There is only the moment. There is no black and white. No good guys or bad guys. There are only periods of time wherein ad hoc alliances can be found. And then the alliances pass. https://www.haaretz.com/opinion/.premium-russia-s-invasion-of-ukraine-could-reshuffle-middle-east-alliances-1.10639694
No, I do NOT advocate we should live like that all the time. Kissinger is a war-criminal after all. But dammit, it keeps being the way things are resolved. Stripped bear of all passion, global geopolitics adheres to realpolitik principles time after time. And once you understand that, you understand why global politics always seems to let us down.
The moral ambiguity of the next few months Right now we see Russia trying to surge to take the cities but Ukraine is ready to resist hard. Unless Ukraine can launch a large and significant counter attack, which can be shown to have rolled up significant Russian gains (and some have advocated they do this), they can’t ‘win’ this war. They can only resist until a diplomatic accord can be reached. https://m.jpost.com/international/article-700438 Meanwhile Russia will have to try and grind out a victory. The Russians will target civilians as they are basically hostages and every death does make it harder for Ukraine to resist. The Ukrainians will hold out for a while as the longer they do the more this cripples Russia economically and geopolitically.
The wider campaign against Russia is now limited to economic and diplomatic channels. We have got some weapons left in the economic war chest... we haven’t sanctioned the Russian Central Bank (which would be the economic version of dropping a nuke). Will we happily make a deal with other nations to get them to pull support for Russia? Will we make deals with other nations to join in our sanctions? I mean, we have started doing so haven’t we? How else do we describe the Americans meeting with Venezuela recently? I mean the USA had Venezuela in its ‘shit list’ for good reason. There was no reason to have talks with Venezuela. Until suddenly there is. https://www.ft.com/content/503e557e-947c-4993-8016-69b2135f4432 ‘What is unimaginable today becomes inevitable tomorrow’.
But THIS has caused a negative reaction from people going ‘how could they do that?’ Let me ask a straight forward question... Should we be willing to make deals with other nations to join us condemning Russia? Make deals to make them join us issuing sanctions upon Russia? Yes? Who? Venezuela? Well we seem to have started that. But then we say that’s too far. So we decide we don’t care for Ukraine enough to support that.
India? Seems easy enough. What do they want to make such a deal? They ain’t gonna join us on a PRINCIPLE. In fact cut price Russian oil would look damn fine to them... what can we offer? Iran? It’s possible. We have the talks in Geneva going on, we can isolate Russia by sweetening it. What about China?
What can we give China to make them siding with us over the Russia-Ukraine issue? No, I don’t mean give them Taiwan, but like the USA could offer to end all the US sanctions on Chinese goods, all those Trump era tariffs, the EU could offer to reduce tariffs on Chinese goods, if they would fully cut off Russia. I’m not saying China would agree... I’m asking would we be willing to try? Or is doing that with China a step to far? A case of a ‘Sorry Ukraine we support you, but we won’t go that far’...
Harsh of me to say so? No. This is why diplomacy and global geopolitics always lets us down. Always breaks our hearts. It’s a cynical and rough world of pragmatic values, where principles only get in the way. China and the US just sat down in Rome and had what are called ‘intense’ talks. Of course they were. https://amp.scmp.com/news/china/diplomacy/article/3170432/us-china-relations-ukraine-casts-long-shadow-national-security It is worth putting everything said about China into context- Russia’s biggest need right now are semi-conductors, and China has none to give. Russia would like financial support, but Chinese banks are in no mood to flush money down the toilet to aid Russia. Russia says ‘we can sell our oil to China’, but it will take years to build the pipelines.
But China is still not blaming Russian. Still quoting Russian propaganda. Russia propaganda is accepted by NO ONE by the way. It’s purpose is to provide nations with the excuse to not join us with sanctions on Russia. They join us if we offer them stuff to join us... and if we don’t offer them a carrot, they can quote Russia by way of saying ‘make us a better offer’. It’s never EVER about principles, about being wrong or right. It’s about dealmaking.
But talks ARE needed. Not just over the fighting in Ukraine, events there are having a global impact already. There are side effects we need to pay real close attention to. Do you know how many countries in North Africa and the Middle East depend on Russian and Ukrainian grain for foodstuffs? Tunisia, Egypt and Lebanon come to mind. Lebanon which is already in the middle of an economic and political crisis? Ouch.
And the last time food princes in this region spiked? We saw the Arab Spring whose net result was Libya and Syria were destroyed as working nation states. We have already lost the next harvest from Ukraine. The longer we wait to make deals, the longer we allow this war continue, the greater the instability it causes globally. https://amp.theguardian.com/global-development/2022/mar/07/we-need-bread-fears-in-middle-east-as-ukraine-russia-war-hits-wheat-imports You think things are crazy NOW? Imagine this war going on and then let’s throw in another Arab Spring? (Blinks) https://www.ft.com/content/02095093-329b-4b66-9f80-13d07350014c
We trying to deal with a humanitarian crisis in Afghanistan, in Yemen, in Libya, in Syria, and now in Ukraine, and all cards on the table... Are we willing to just allow market forces dictate food prices and which nations get food, knowing that suddenly skyrocketing food prices is like pouring petrol upon something and then hoping there isn’t a spark? Food protests have already began in Iraq and we ain’t even a month into this war... https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/9/iraq-protest-food-prices-russia-ukraine-war
How bad do things have to get before we sit down with everyone and anyone and make some deals? A more unstable world endangers us all. In terms of realpolitik right now the most important thing our nations can do is whatever is required to keep things from spiralling out of control. But we ain’t. Pragmatism really stops us from getting our virtue signalling flex you know? Peace talks have already risen to involve foreign ministers (the recent ones in Turkey). Ukraine is already offering a kinda of neutrality in writing to get Russia to leave; a quiet kind of surrender based on the fact that despite the EU saying Ukraine can join, really, they don’t want them. https://www.npr.org/2022/03/11/1086007627/european-union-ukraine-membership-fast-track There are no easy answers.
How our world has changed... Hey, you would be totally within your rights to describe everything I am saying as cynical, alarmist and over the top. And I don’t blame you. But... think about it this way. What was the geopolitical reality of the world 24 hours before Putin sent his troops in? It was only just over two weeks ago.
1- NATO was split politically; we had Germany and France not agreeing with the Eastern European members and working to maintain peace at any cost. 2- Russia and China had a working relationship based over a dozen years of good terms and China was saying ‘Russia has a point’ repeatedly. 3- Within western nations Russian disinformation narratives were given credence and backed up by many on BOTH the left wing and the right wing. 4- Russia’s influence in the Middle East was strong. 5- Putin has no serious threat to his rule. Had he NOT invaded, he would have had this as his situation. He could have played this for years. In fact this was why I honestly believed he wasn’t gonna go in. Why I said so. From the point of view of geopolitics? Putin held a strong hand.
And then he invaded. He proved US Intelligence was right all along. And now? 1- NATO has unified in a way that I honestly thought was impossible a month ago. Holy shit. And Germany saying they were building up their armed forces- you don’t get it. Germany’s armed forces were in a woeful state. Germany’s massive spend on defence is a HUGE 180. https://nationalinterest.org/blog/reboot/germanys-military-has-some-serious-problems-193129 And https://www.france24.com/en/live-news/20220314-germany-to-buy-dozens-of-us-fighter-jets-in-spending-spree 2- China refuses to support Putin, its banks won’t lend him money, they not sticking out their neck for him. China is clearly being very, very quiet... they talking about how bad sanctions are for the global economy and how it’s NATO’s fault, but so far not supporting Russia one jot. So far... They COULD support him. But only if we decide we won’t try and split the two. And that is our choice.
3- Putins narratives were rejected and he was hit by sanctions that are ripping his already precarious economy over the edge. And the sanctions on Russia on a scale from 1 to 10 are currently at a 7. Maybe an 8. We can do more. 4- Russians influence In the Middle East is being questioned seriously... I mean what if he can’t get weapons to those Russian jets in Syria? 5- Russia is running out of space in jail for the people arrested protesting the war. Expect bans on foreign travel. And maybe gulags.
As someone who writes about geopolitical moves and countermoves and who has spent the last three years sharing that with Imgur users allow me just say... The war in Ukraine has opened up the possibility of change in the entire global geopolitical narrative. Two weeks ago NO ONE predicted those five things. We have a concrete example of how one war has changed everything and the longer it goes on the more likely radical change will happen. While our hearts long for simple answers, our heads must remain open to the idea that nothing is ever simple. Nothing.
The future? Yet for all of this, NO ONE knows what our future will be. No one. Not Zelensky, not Biden, not Xi, not Putin, not Macron, not Boris. Not you, certainly not me, and equally certainly, not any opinion writer, journalist, talking head or expert. We don’t know. Folks are speculating to get their exclusive ‘I told you so T-Shirts’ but no, no one knows. When you start a war, any war, you write the opening line of the story. After that? After that the story will write itself. Literally _anything_ can happen and I believe two weeks into this war we are seeing it.
The only certainty, the only real certainty, is that this storm? This too will pass. The fighting will stop. The shells will stop falling. And there will be a peace- be it a month from now or a year from now or ten years from now. But the longer it goes on, the more things will change. The less simple things will become. And that given that anything can happen, we need to mentally prepare for the fact that ‘Ukraine loses this war’ is not even close to being the worst thing that can happen. Prepare now so we are not shocked. Mentally get ready for a world that could be much more unstable.
But what do I know? I am just a little dude on Imgur. I could be wrong. I am worried about all of this but I am not afraid. The problems of the world are staggering and seemingly insurmountable but I look to what we have overcome in our past and I retain faith in humanity. I see the groundswell of passion, of support for Ukraine. I see the millions in cash raised by normal everyday men and women. And I retain faith in humanity.
I see the people helping others in Yeman, in Syria, in Ukraine, in Afghanistan... I write about the actions of men with guns because that is what drives the news, but that doesn’t mean their opposites are not out there. There are people today all over the world, who are caring for the refugee, the injured, the hungry, the victims. And these amazing people are legion. And I retain faith in humanity. Hope is not a campaign slogan. It is an act of defiance against forces beyond us all. It is a positive affirmation of a belief in life, all life. It is shield against unbearably bad news and a sword to cut through the endless noise of a billion voices all clamouring to be heard. Hope is a weapon.
Notes: Sorry if I annoyed anyone. I am torn between a battle between my heart and my head. My heart wants Ukraine to win, and Putin to lose and for good to be good and for bad to be bad. My head tells me the world doesn’t work that and that it’s much messier and filled with stupid decisions. But while the news can be grim, this is where I find my inspiration. The people who work tirelessly to help and bring us news... from Yemen https://reliefweb.int/report/yemen/yemen-food-security-nutrition-snapshot-march-2022-enar https://reliefweb.int/report/yemen/yemen-humanitarian-fund-2021-annual-monitoring-report https://www.rescue.org/press-release/irc-calls-donors-fully-fund-yemens-humanitarian-response-plan-war-approaches-7-year?amp From Libya https://reliefweb.int/report/libya/libya-humanitarian-response-plan-2022-december-2021-enar https://reliefweb.int/report/algeria/humanitarian-aid-eu-allocates-18-million-algeria-egypt-and-libya From Syria: https://www.middleeastmonitor.com/20220225-un-14-6m-need-humanitarian-assistance-in-syria/amp/ https://www.rescue.org/press-release/syria-11-years-hunger-drought-and-collapsing-economy-threaten-even-more-misery?amp While each of these contains news of problems these nations face, they are also products of vast support networks and thousands upon thousands of human beings helping, talking, caring for one another. I do not mention these to cause guilt or shame. I mention them to celebrate those people, to call upon others to celebrate them. That as we saw during COVID, we focus on the negative because it’s easier to do so, but that the positive is all around us. Selfless, humble, and helping. People being amazing. If the world seems dark, fix on that. Fix on the basic decency of humans, on our amazing capacity for compassion. It’s a powerful weapon indeed. Anyway- Hey, I do this stuff because I love it and that’s that. But if you feel like buying me a coffee for it, you can click on the link below. I’m gonna avoid Ukraine now for a bit. There are other things happening in the world we are missing. https://www.buymeacoffee.com/Thefeck A full list of the posts done on Imgur over the years. Including a LOT about the Middle East and Russia over the years. https://imgur.com/gallery/aTASLQH
item.dc:creator: thefeckamisigningupwithfacebook

item.title: Jen Psaki dropping another truth bomb (with sound)

When asked about the recently announced sanctions issued by Russia against her and other individuals, Jen responded with a little bit of amusement.
Note that Hunter Biden is on the list for whatever reason along with former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.

item.title: Comparisons
item.description:
How many Earths fit inside the Sun.
Tornado vs wind farm.
Small vs Medium OJ at McDonalds.
Different lightbulbs in East and West Berlin.
Before and after hurricane Florence.
Human vs Your Mom. (or a Gorilla).
NBA player vs reporter.
Smoker vs non-smoker.
WBA players and their cheerleaders.
Chinese explorer Zheng's ship vs Columbus ship.
Modern tamato vs one grown from a 150 year old seed.

item.title: My heart. My soul.
item.description: No idea where this originally came from or any specific information. It's apparently an old man meeting his grandchild for the first time. The mum touches his knees as a sign of respect, then quickly makes sure baby is supported. But there's no way he'd drop them. It's just a beautiful, once in a lifetime moment that we get to share. Most Viral edit: Just be cool to people. Hug your cats, dogs, kids, calves, rabbits, geckos, unspeakable horrors, guinea pigs or isopods. Send me the things that make you happy beyond description.
item.dc:creator: Mugworttea

item.title: "But trans people are a dangerous new fad..."
item.description:
Christine Jorgensen. Soldier, author, race car driver, activist, actress, singer, journalist, and first American trans woman to receive gender confirmation surgery.
"As you can see by the enclosed photos, taken just before the operation, I have changed a great deal. But it is the other changes that are so much more important. Remember the shy, miserable person who left America? Well, that person is no more and, as you can see, I'm in marvelous spirits." - Christine Jorgensen in a letter to friends dated October 8, 1951
item.dc:creator: ThatOtherGirlYouKnow

item.title: Ukrainian man was angry and return to fight against Russian army because his son was killed by Russian army.
item.dc:creator: SavingHongKong

item.title: Carefully curated, easy-to-read Ukraine updates with sources, for people who want to be informed but don't have time.
item.description:
Russia, however, has not been present for the hearings. The impact of this is little (if any at all) for most Russians, but officials could be branded war criminals, and in the future would be arrested if they set foot in (the more respectable) UN member countries. https://www.usnews.com/news/world/articles/2022-03-14/world-court-to-rule-in-ukraine-case-against-russia-on-march-16 https://www.reuters.com/world/world-court-rule-as-soon-possible-ukrainian-demands-against-russia-2022-03-07/ https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/7/russia-snubs-un-court-hearings-in-case-brought-by-ukraine
This particular one that was shot down, a Russian Forpost done, flew over Poland before entering Ukraine's airspace and being shot down. Apparently, this does not constitute an attack on NATO; additional actions aren't considered at this time. https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-ato/3429939-russian-drones-keep-breaching-nato-airspace.html https://news.yahoo.com/putin-drone-shot-down-after-flying-into-nato-airspace-100345543.html https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/russian-drone-entered-polish-airspace-bvkzk9kpj
https://kyivindependent.com/uncategorized/ukraines-military-over-13500-russian-troops-have-been-killed-since-feb-24/ Obviously, this is coming from a completely Ukrainian source, but so far appears to be accurate. Russia is not releasing information regarding their losses, but their overtures to China for aid (described later in this post) reveal the truth.
Little to no advancements have been made over the weekend, and it's not like these guys are taking Sat/Sunday off work. Russian ground forces are still ~9 miles NW of Kyiv, virtually unchanged since Thursday, and their eastern lines 12-19 miles away are also stalled. There appears to be an attempt to bypass Kharkiv and connect with forces in southern Ukraine to cut off aid to the eastern front. https://www.forbes.com/sites/joewalsh/2022/03/14/russian-forces-stalled-outside-kyiv-us-says/?sh=60027cee6864 https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2022/03/14/ukraine-russia-invasion-live-updates/7031496001/ https://www.euronews.com/2022/03/15/pentagon-most-of-russia-s-military-offensives-remain-stalled
This is coming from spokesman for (and generals in) both the Ukrainian and U.S. military, an area I think we can consider them experts in. Russia is rapidly running out of munitions and rations. https://kyivindependent.com/uncategorized/retired-us-army-general-believes-russia-will-exhaust-ability-to-fight-within-ten-days/ https://www.msn.com/en-gb/news/world/putin-s-forces-may-only-be-able-to-keep-up-fight-for-another-14-days/ar-AAV3vNR?li=BBoPRmx&srcref=rss https://www.thesun.co.uk/news/17753076/russia-ukraine-war-live-updates-kyiv-vladimir-putin-news/
Russia requested SAMs, drones, armored vehicles, intelligence equipment, et cetera. U.S. intelligence reports indicate that China has expressed openness to providing Russia with arms, food, and cash, with some claiming that they've already decided to do so... https://www.cnn.com/2022/03/14/politics/us-china-russia-ukraine/index.html https://www.ft.com/content/52ea7aab-f8d1-46b6-9d66-18545c5ef9b9 https://www.reuters.com/article/uk-ukraine-crisis-usa-china-cable/u-s-told-nato-asia-allies-china-willing-to-supply-arms-to-russia-u-s-official-idUKKCN2LB27Shttps://www.theguardian.com/world/2022/mar/15/china-has-already-decided-to-send-economic-aid-to-russia-in-ukraine-conflict-us-officials-fear ...But simultaneously, reports have come out that indicate China wants absolutely nothing to do with Russia's invasion unless they somehow succeed: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/3/15/china-does-not-want-to-be-impacted-by-russia-sanctions-fm https://www.usnews.com/news/world-report/articles/2022-03-14/china-signals-disinterest-in-providing-weapons-to-russia-for-brutal-ukraine-campaign If by some chance they covertly supply Russia with gear, I'd expect to start seeing it in the hands of Ukrainian soldiers after Russia gets more asses kicked.
Russia hasn't defaulted since the Bolshevik revolution over 100 years ago. This is the first payment due since the invasion began, and the ruble has plummeted in value - plus, those they owe debts to may not even accept the payments in rubles. Defaulting on their debts would have huge economic impacts, comparable to the sanctions themselves. https://www.cnn.com/2022/03/14/investing/russia-economy-default/index.html https://www.nytimes.com/2022/03/15/business/russia-debt-bonds-default.html https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/can-russia-pay-its-creditors-what-happens-if-not-2022-03-15/ https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FxKZ2rS2-Dc&t=1s&ab_channel=CNBCTelevision
The new law allows them seize planes owned by other countries that are refusing to do business in Russia (or can't, due to sanctions). These planes value at roughly ~$10,000,000,000. https://www.businessinsider.com/russia-is-seizing-hundreds-of-lessor-planes-poses-risk-passengers-2022-3 https://www.cnbc.com/2022/03/14/putin-allows-russian-airlines-to-fly-10-billion-worth-of-foreign-owned-planes.html https://www.foxnews.com/us/putin-aircraft-sanctions-russian-aviation
They're traveling via train, apparently. The Polish government said in a statement that the visit was intended "to confirm the unequivocal support of the entire European Union for the sovereignty and independence of Ukraine and to present a broad package of support for the Ukrainian state and society". https://www.cnn.com/europe/live-news/ukraine-russia-putin-news-03-15-22/h_1a1f1bda4ee04c635fcd7d03891d2c3a https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/3-eu-leaders-travel-to-kyiv-in-show-of-support-for-ukraine https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-60748234 Being a target like a prime minister and rolling into an active warzone in a train takes some pretty big balls, if you ask me. AS YOU ALWAYS SHOULD, if you see any issues with the sources, have better sources, or information refuting it here, share it with everyone, and I will make updates where necessary.
item.dc:creator: VikingWeightlifting

item.title: The double entendres are real in this video
item.description: Front page edit Go watch this man!!! He gladly educates the community on personal security and his skills are unparalleled. https://youtube.com/c/lockpickinglawyer
item.dc:creator: zMike70z
